channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tornadoes, wildfires and blinding dust sweep across US as massive storm leaves at least 35 dead
item.description: PIEDMONT, Mo. (AP) — Residents pounded by unusually vicious weather across parts of the U.S. surveyed damage Sunday from violent tornadoes, high winds and blinding dust storms that decimated homes and other structures and left at least 35 people dead.
item.dc:creator: JEFF ROBERSON, REBECCA REYNOLDS and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Washington man accused of having enough fentanyl to kill 278,000 people after getting clemency from Dem gov
item.description: Percy Levy, 54, is facing nearly a dozen charges related to drugs and weapon possession in Snohomish County, Washington, according to inmate records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Average tax refunds are up in 2025 so far, total early returns down
item.description: The average tax refund amount is higher than it was last year at this time, according to a batch of data released by the IRS late last week.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: At least 33 dead as tornadoes, wildfires, and blinding dust sweep across US
item.description: Violent tornadoes and high winds decimated homes, wiped out schools and toppled semitractor-trailers as a monster storm that also produced dust storms and icy conditions killed at least 33 people across the central and southern U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Carolina spring break murder victim's family awarded $700M in civil suit
item.description: A South Carolina jury awarded $700 million to the family of Brittanee Drexel, who was kidnapped and murdered on a 2009 spring break trip to Myrtle Beach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan judge's order for Walmart shoplifters to wash cars squashed by his boss
item.description: A Michigan judge sought to have Walmart shoplifters offer free car washes for their punishment, but the plan was overruled by a senior jurist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing American student linked to popular nightclub known for extravagant parties, hours before disappearance
item.description: Venmo transactions from Sudiksha Konanki's account suggest the spring breaker may have partied at Punta Cana's 'Coco Bongo' in the days before she went missing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tornadoes, wildfires and blinding dust sweep across US as massive storm leaves at least 35 dead
item.description: Residents pounded by unusually vicious weather across parts of the U.S. surveyed damage Sunday from violent tornadoes, high winds and blinding dust storms that decimated homes and other structures and left at least 35 people dead.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Roberson, Rebecca Reynolds And Julie Walker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Indiana cop charged after forcing daughter, 6, to stand in freezing cold with threatening sign
item.description: An Indiana police officer allegedly forced his six-year-old daughter to stand outside in cold temperatures holding a sign threatening her brother as punishment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: St. Patrick's Day parade celebrates Boston heritage in America’s most Irish big city
item.description: It’s that time of year again when more than a million revelers fill the streets of America’s most Irish big city for the South Boston St. Patrick’s Day parade.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Alien Enemies Act: What to know about a 1798 law that Trump has invoked for deportations
item.description: President Donald Trump has invoked the Alien Enemies Act for the first time since World War II, granting himself sweeping powers under a centuries-old law to deport people associated with a Venezuelan gang.
item.dc:creator: Tim Sullivan And Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump invokes Alien Enemies Act in mass deportation effort
item.description: The president’s move comes hours after a judge temporarily blocked the removal of 5 Venezuelans who the Trump administration says are members of Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: US strikes Houthis in Yemen as Trump warns Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the United States military to "launch decisive and powerful" action against the Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Fetterman claps back at Ocasio-Cortez on government funding vote
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.), who announced days ago that he would vote for a House-passed funding bill to avoid a government shutdown, said he doesn’t care about firebrand Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s (D-N.Y.) harsh criticism of Democratic senators who will vote to advance the controversial bill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Bulls title banners removed for season after sustaining damage during Disturbed concert
item.description: All six Bulls championship banners have been removed from the United Center for the rest of the season after sustaining damage during a concert.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Monster storm across the US sparks threat of tornadoes and fire, killing at least 16
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — The threat of violent tornadoes in parts of the U.S. proved deadly as well as destructive as whipping winds moved east into the Mississippi Valley and Deep South on Saturday, with at least 16 people killed and scores of homes decimated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The line-swapping hack at Disney, Universal and Six Flags that parents need to know
item.description: Are some of your children too short, too young, or just too terrified to accompany you on your favorite theme park rides? Don’t let it spoil everyone’s vacation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Another state looks to ban this common question drivers are asked every day
item.description: A bill has been introduced into the Colorado legislature that would prohibit officers from asking "Do you know why I pulled you over," and instead tell the driver why they are conducting the traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, KRON4

item.title: Extreme weather expected Saturday gets rarely used 'high risk' designation
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A dynamic storm system threatening to spawn powerful tornadoes and hail as big as baseballs has earned a relatively rare designation from forecasters: A “high risk” day of severe weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Heartwarming photos of Trump with Musk’s son 'Lil X' go viral
item.description: Heartwarming photographs of President Donald Trump walking to Marine Force One with Elon Musk’s son are going viral, with internet users doting over the joyful moment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The line-swapping hack at Disney, Universal and Six Flags that all parents should know about
item.description: Are some of your children too short, too young, or just too terrified to accompany you on your favorite theme park rides? If so, it doesn't have to slow you down.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voters flood town halls with fears of Social Security cuts, putting heat on GOP over Musk and DOGE
item.description: HOLLAND, Mich. (AP) — One after another, callers on a telephone town hall with U.S. Rep. Bill Huizenga pressed the Michigan Republican about possible cuts to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At least 32 dead in massive US storm after new fatalities reported in Kansas and Mississippi
item.description: At least 32 people have been killed in a monster storm sweeping across much of the U.S. Kansas authorities said eight people died in a highway pileup caused by a dust storm Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Russ Bynum And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar thinks Selena 'partly to blame' for death: Report
item.description: Saldívar, 64, officially filed for parole in January. She shot Selena during a confrontation in a Texas hotel room.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech Friday at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances about the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have o
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate votes to avoid government shutdown
item.description: The Senate voted mostly along party lines Friday afternoon to pass the House Republican-drafted bill to fund government through September, avoiding a government shutdown only hours before funding was due to lapse. President Trump is expected to sign the bill into law. The final vote was 54-46.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Parents arrested after refusing child a ride to school; found walking on highway
item.description: The Midland Police Department arrested two people earlier this month after a young girl was found walking alone on a busy street in the early morning hours.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Meza, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration resumes detention of immigrant families after Biden-era pause
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration resumed family detention of immigrants last week in a South Texas facility after a Biden-era pause, a legal nonprofit group providing services to migrant families said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
